codeReview.summary: The commit cleanly repositions the public CRUD surface around model-level Store methods and the Crud trait, while demoting Repo to an internal/advanced seam in both code docs and README. The macro change adds inherent save/save_many entrypoints that align implementation with the documented recommendation, and the compile-surface proof covers the newly recommended call sites.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill does not currently say that a pure API-surface/documentation refactor can be validated by compile-surface proof plus docs alignment without adding new runtime regressions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges explicitly requests this guidance, and this feature's diff is limited to README.md, rustdoc comments, derive-generated inherent methods in macros/src/lib.rs, and compile-surface coverage in core/tests/facade_exports.rs.

summary: Pass. The reviewed feature matches its scope: it clarifies the recommended public CRUD API, de-emphasizes Repo as an internal helper layer, and adds generated model-level save/save_many methods plus compile-surface evidence so the documented path is real. I found no blocking implementation gaps in the reviewed commit.
